title: Engineer, Electrical-Benoi
description:
Responsibilities

Productivity

  • Deploy and effective utilization of manpower and material resources in the department. In addition, liaising with other departments on daily production schedule.
  • Assist in Planning of department manpower projections
  • Assess and plan weekly manpower deployment and allocation of repair vessel to Sub-contractor.
  • Obtain and monitor drawings/ technical information.
  • Attend assigned project progress meeting/HAT meeting.
  • Assess staff work performance under his charge as required.
  • Identify training needs for his men.
  • Monitor job progress and man hours of the assigned project. Ensure no delays to project schedule and work within assigned budget.
  • Study and evaluate the work processes and methods of the department and explore new ideas to improve work quality and productivity.
  • Liaise with owners representative and/or class surveyor during witness/inspection.
  • Raising of Sub-Contractor order form.
  • Check and monitor of Sub-contractor deployment.
  • Raise PR or DRL Out Materials required for the vessel and monitoring of Material usage/availability.
  • Assist in reviewing Dept Documentation and Processes.
  • Preparation for VSCC meeting

a)   Highlight areas need to other trades to clear their work (e.g. temporary lightings, staging, closing of access, etc.

c)    Updates/ highlight any housekeeping and safety issues

Quality

  • Evaluate technical/ engineering requirements of the jobs, and reviews drawings.
  • Resolve any technical problems or issues with Designer, contractors, class & owner.
  • Monitor and ensure quality work is produced at targeted schedule

Safety

  • Conduct RA assessment/ BBS observation/ hazard reporting
  • Participate in department safety work plan, attend monthly EHS safety community meeting.
  • Conduct activities which met regulatory requirements
  • Attend VSCC Meetings

Requirement

  • Degree in Electrical or Electronic Engineering
  • Preferably 3 – 5 years of working experiences
  • Work in marine industry or similar capacity
  • Knowledge of ship’s electrical system, control system and their classification requirements.
  • Able to interpret technical/electrical drawings and specification.

Other Information:

  • Location: Benoi Road (Island-wide transport provided)
  • 5 days work week
  • Working Hours: 7.25am-5pm

postedCompany.description:
We provide innovative solutions in turnkey building, repair and conversion services for naval and commercial vessels and are one of the few shipyards in the world capable of supporting the entire lifecycle of a ship. Our shipyards in Singapore and the U.S. have established a strong track record in designing and building highly customised and technically sophisticated commercial and military vessels for customers worldwide.


Expanding on our core competencies in large scale engineering, we provide a comprehensive suite of environmental engineering solutions in water, wastewater, solid waste and renewable energy. A trusted partner to naval and commercial sector customers worldwide, our track record includes successfully delivered projects in Brunei, China, India, Thailand, Singapore, the Middle East and the U.S.
postedCompany.name: ST ENGINEERING MARINE LTD.
